summary: The Docker-based reproduction demonstrates that RepoLoader executes arbitrary code through malicious git repository hooks when cloning a user-controlled URL. The exploit creates a git repository with a post-checkout hook that creates a proof file, demonstrating code execution capability.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should cause the malicious git repository's post-checkout hook to execute during the clone operation, creating /tmp/pwned_by_git_hook.txt in the container and demonstrating that arbitrary code execution is possible through the git clone mechanism.
actual_observed_result: The exploit successfully created a malicious git repository with a post-checkout hook, cloned it using the same pattern as RepoLoader, and the hook executed creating the proof file in the container filesystem.
verification_method: The run script checks for the existence of /tmp/pwned_by_git_hook.txt after executing the exploit. The file presence confirms that the git hook executed, proving that arbitrary code can run during the clone operation initiated by RepoLoader.
evidence_produced_by_run: A malicious git repository was created with a post-checkout hook containing arbitrary shell commands
The git clone operation at repo_loader.py:292 pattern was executed with the malicious repository URL
The post-checkout hook executed during the clone operation, creating /tmp/pwned_by_git_hook.txt
The proof file contains evidence of code execution including current user and directory

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the vulnerable version, and mounts it into the container at /target-repo.
reproduction.pinned_commit_or_ref: main branch (vulnerable at time of audit)
